label: CMS — Sanity
summary: Use when a Next.js App Router site should read structured content from Sanity, with optional draft preview / Presentation Tool Visual Editing. Ships only frontend integration glue — env config, a lazy public+draft client factory, a query helper, and paired draft-mode enable/disable routes. When Sanity env is missing or a preview placeholder, Sanity-backed pages must render the shipped seedContent plus a discreet SanityConfigNotice instead of crashing.
summarySv: Kopplar sajten till Sanity, ett redigeringsverktyg där du uppdaterar texter och bilder utan att röra koden. Välj det när innehållet ska kunna ändras löpande av dig eller en redaktör; tills riktiga nycklar läggs in körs demoläge med färdigt exempelinnehåll, utan delad lagring.

envVars.purpose: Sanity project ID that identifies which project to read content from. Find it in sanity.io/manage or the project's sanity.config.ts. Tagged feature-runtime because the placeholder-aware isSanityConfigured() gates all Sanity access: pages render seedContent + SanityConfigNotice, and the draft-mode routes answer 503 instead of crashing.
Sanity dataset to query, usually 'production'. Find it in the Sanity project's dataset settings. Same feature-runtime fallback as NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID.

Pinned Sanity API version date (e.g. 2025-09-25) for stable query behavior across Sanity API changes. Optional — the client falls back to a pinned default when unset, so an empty value is not even warning-worthy.

URL where the separate Sanity Studio is hosted; used for stega edit-intent links and the Presentation Tool. Optional — defaults to the local Studio dev URL, only relevant once draft/editing flows are wired up.

Server-only Sanity token with read access, needed for draft-mode preview and any private dataset. Create it in the Sanity project's API settings (Manage -> API -> Tokens). Tagged feature-runtime: the placeholder-aware isSanityDraftTokenConfigured() gates the draft client, and the draft-mode enable route answers 503 when it is missing or a stub; published-content reads keep working.

notes: Legacy import Fas D: promoted from the sanity-cms-alt draft (open decision #2) — alt shipped the paired disable route + sanityFetch helper, and its exposes named real exports unlike the primary draft. Fixes: non-throwing api.ts + placeholder-aware isSanityConfigured(), lazy public/draft client split (CDN vs server-only token), mock:seed via seedContent + SanityConfigNotice, manual disable route (next-sanity ships no disableDraftMode export). lastVerified = legacy scrape date until first preview-test (no-false-green).
